title: [BUG] various methods’ param names `index`, `indexOrName`, `indexOrKey` in `index.d.ts` are misleading (1-indexed)
author: lionel-rowe
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

param names `index`, `indexOrName`, and `indexOrKey` in `index.d.ts` are misleading, due to being 1-indexed.

Some of these methods specify "1-indexed" in the JSDoc, but others don't. JS is 0-indexed, so params named "index" are usually understood to be 0-indexed.

Example:

https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/blob/5bed18b45e824f409b08456b59b87430ded023ab/index.d.ts#L1761-L1764

For a newly created workbook with 1 sheet, `wb.getWorksheet(0)` doesn't get the first worksheet, `wb.getWorksheet(1)` does. Meanwhile, `wb.worksheets[0]` gets the first sheet as expected. The JSDoc for `getWorksheet` isn't much help, as it contradicts the param name (is it an ID or an index?)

Lib version: 4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce

Call one of the affected methods from TypeScript and view intellisense docs.

## The expected behaviour:

Param names/JSDoc to be clearer.

## Possible solution (optional, but very helpful):

Would be better to use `number`/`num` where 1-indexed, or `id` if it's an ID.
